hyperedges.relation: YAML-driven mod loading pipeline: mods declare metadata and stages in YAML, ModLoader fetches and merges them
BG3-style turn-based combat subsystem: engage → initiative → player/enemy turns → status effects → resolution
Deterministic run progression: seed-based stage selection via depth bands and run planner ensures reproducible runs
Roguelike economy loop: carry loot during runs, extract to persistent stash, spend at town shop
Multi-tier test architecture: contracts validate YAML schemas, unit tests cover logic, sandbox tests mutate singletons, e2e tests verify full game flow — all enforced by CI
Visibility triad: fog, light, and sight systems interact to determine what the player can see on the map
Six core D&D 5e classes forming the character progression system
Six core D&D 5e races providing ability bonuses and racial traits
Four-tier mod complexity ladder: data → tuning → YAML hooks → JS scripts, designed so most mods stay at tiers 1-3
BG3-style UI shell: index.html defines DOM layout for side panel, hotbar, combat log, dice overlay, and resource pips
Procedural map generation using BSP or cellular automata with deterministic RNG streams
Three independent RNG streams prevent cross-system contamination of deterministic seeds
Seed selects storyline which configures generator type, biome theme, and encounter parameters for the entire run
Town-dungeon-town loop with extraction banking on success and item/gold loss on death
BG3-style combat flow: engage opener → on-hit start combat → roll initiative → turn actions → flee or victory
Four foundational architecture decisions made on 2026-04-16 establishing the project's technical direction
Three collaborating visibility systems controlling what the player can see on the map
Macro-floor approach creates 3 large acts with distinct biomes and distributed rest points for Diablo-2-scale exploration
All entities (player, hostile, friendly) share the same D&D 5e race/class framework with faction-driven behavior
Pool of seed-selectable map blueprint scenarios providing distinct layouts, AI behaviors, and objectives
Tightly coupled visibility subsystems sharing LOS, light level, and fog alpha data
Standalone singletons (not mixins) wired together for scripted event/dialog/flag state management
Core gameplay loop systems that interact during combat mode turns
Four-layer test pyramid from fast schema checks to full browser integration
Sprite sources with procedural fallback for missing art; all render at 48x48 display size
Infrastructure-as-code pipeline: OpenTofu provisions AWS resources, OIDC authenticates CI, CloudFront serves static builds
Complete AI state machine with defined transitions for enemy behavior lifecycle
BG3-inspired UI panels composing the game HUD
